panels.description: Stacked per-model breakdown of cache_read and cache_write spend. Anthropic and Bedrock-Anthropic models show both series; OpenAI shows cache_read only (no write fee). Replaces the old token-throughput view — same shape, but priced so you can see where cache dollars actually concentrate.
panels.title: Cache Spend by Model ($/hour)

panels.description: Per-model breakdown of token throughput for models not in the canonical pricing list. Add them to the pricing list to start tracking cost.
panels.title: Untracked Token Throughput by Model

panels.description: Per-request distribution of cached prefix size (input_cache_read tokens). This is the prompt prefix being reused on each turn — large system prompts, loaded skills, MCP tool definitions, etc. p95 jumps when context bloats; investigate by checking which skills/tools were added since the jump.
panels.title: Cache Read Tokens Per Request (Context Size)

panels.description: Per-request distribution of cache write size (input_cache_write tokens). First request of a session pays a large cache_write to populate the prefix; steady-state turns should be small or zero. Persistent large p95 means the cache is being invalidated and rewritten — usually because the 'stable' prefix isn't actually stable (skill order changing, tool defs drifting, etc.).
panels.title: Cache Write Tokens Per Request (Cache Churn)

panels.description: p95 cached prefix size broken out by response model. Identifies which model/workload is responsible for context bloat (e.g. opus carrying 127k cached tokens per turn while mini carries 4k). Use this to target which skill/MCP loadouts to trim.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, gen_ai_response_model) (rate(agentgateway_gen_ai_client_token_usage_bucket{namespace=~"$namespace", gen_ai_token_type="input_cache_read", gen_ai_response_model!="unknown", gen_ai_response_model!=""}[$__rate_interval])))

* 100
